seo.title: 100 Prompt Engineering resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Prompt Engineering resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource guide provides developers with the specific patterns, evaluation frameworks, and implementation tools required to move beyond basic chat interfaces and build reliable, production-grade LLM applications. It focuses on systematic prompt engineering, structured output enforcement, and regression testing.

items.title: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Reasoning
items.description: Instruct the model to 'think step-by-step' before providing a final answer to improve performance on logic and math tasks.

items.title: Few-Shot In-Context Learning
items.description: Provide 3-5 high-quality examples of input-output pairs within the prompt to define specific formatting and stylistic requirements.

items.title: XML Tagging for Context Separation
items.description: Use tags like <context>, <instruction>, and <examples> to help models like Claude 3.5 Sonnet distinguish between metadata and task data.

items.title: Chain-of-Verification (CoVe)
items.description: A multi-step process where the model generates a response, identifies its own potential errors, and verifies facts before final output.

items.title: Skeleton-of-Thought (SoT)
items.description: Prompt the model to generate a high-level outline first, then expand each section to reduce latency and improve structural coherence.

items.title: Negative Constraints and Guardrails
items.description: Explicitly list forbidden words, topics, or formats (e.g., 'Do not use markdown') to prevent unwanted output behavior.

items.title: Persona-Based System Prompts
items.description: Define a specific professional role (e.g., 'You are a Senior Site Reliability Engineer') to prime the model for specific technical jargon and tone.

items.title: Self-Consistency Sampling
items.description: Generate multiple outputs for the same prompt and use a majority vote or LLM-as-a-judge to select the most accurate result.

items.title: Dynamic Context Window Management
items.description: Using RAG or sliding windows to pass only the most relevant 20-30% of document context to avoid the 'lost in the middle' phenomenon.

items.title: Output Formatting via Pydantic
items.description: Force the model to return valid JSON by providing a strict schema, often implemented via libraries like Instructor.

items.title: Promptfoo CLI
items.description: A matrix testing tool that allows you to run prompts against multiple models and check outputs against predefined assertions.

items.title: LangSmith (LangChain)
items.description: A platform for tracing, debugging, and evaluating LLM applications with integrated dataset management for regression testing.

items.title: Braintrust
items.description: An enterprise-grade stack for logging LLM calls, managing prompt versions, and running automated evaluation workflows.

items.title: DeepEval (Pytest for LLMs)
items.description: A Python framework for unit testing LLM outputs based on metrics like faithfulness, relevance, and hallucination scores.

items.title: Helicone
items.description: An open-source observability proxy that tracks costs, latency, and token usage for OpenAI and Anthropic requests.

items.title: PromptLayer
items.description: A middleware for logging and versioning prompts, allowing developers to roll back to previous prompt iterations without code changes.

items.title: Giskard
items.description: An open-source library for detecting vulnerabilities, hallucinations, and biases in LLM-based applications.

items.title: Honeycomb LLM Observability
items.description: Distributed tracing for LLMs to understand the latency impact of each step in a complex multi-agent chain.

items.title: Weights & Biases Prompts
items.description: A suite of tools for visualizing and inspecting the execution flow of LLM chains and pipelines.

items.title: Arize Phoenix
items.description: Open-source observability for RAG and LLM applications, focusing on embedding visualization and retrieval evaluation.

items.title: Instructor Library
items.description: The industry standard for getting structured data (JSON) from LLMs using Pydantic models for Python or Zod for TS.

items.title: Vercel AI SDK (generateObject)
items.description: A unified interface for generating type-safe JSON objects across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Gemini models.

items.title: Outlines (by .txt)
items.description: A library for neural text generation that uses regex and context-free grammars to guarantee 100% valid JSON or code.

items.title: LMQL (Language Model Query Language)
items.description: A programming language that combines logic programming with LLM generation to constrain output at the token level.

items.title: Guidance (Microsoft)
items.description: A template language for controlling LLMs, allowing you to interleave generation, control flow, and fixed strings.

items.title: Marvin AI
items.description: A lightweight library that uses LLMs to power standard Python functions, handling the prompt engineering under the hood.

items.title: TypeChat
items.description: Microsoft's library for replacing complex prompt engineering with schema-based type definitions to guide model output.

items.title: Jsonformer
items.description: A library specifically designed to fill in the values of a JSON schema while the structure is pre-defined, saving tokens.

items.title: SGLang
items.description: A fast backend and frontend for structured generation, optimized for high-throughput serving and complex control flow.

items.title: Portkey AI Gateway
items.description: A control plane to manage multiple LLMs with built-in retries, fallbacks, and load balancing across providers.

pro_tips: Use XML tags for Anthropic Claude models to separate instructions from input data; it significantly reduces instruction-following failures.
Implement 'Prompt Caching' on supported models (Anthropic, Gemini) to reduce costs and latency by up to 90% for long system instructions.
Avoid using superlatives like 'be creative' or 'be amazing'; instead, provide quantitative metrics or specific examples of what 'good' looks like.
Always specify the exact model version (e.g., gpt-4o-2024-05-13) in production to avoid silent regressions when providers update their aliases.
Log your 'LLM-as-a-judge' scores alongside human evaluations to calibrate your automated testing suite before relying on it for CI/CD.
